blocks.content: Their block was the one just before they had to split off — Daisy on Magnolia, Kit on the street past it. They always slowed down here.
Mrs. Jones was on the front porch, watchful and warm, looking like a woman who had been watching Kit and Daisy do exactly this for four years.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Lord Almighty. You get caught in that?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yes ma'am. Hardware store overhang on Cedar.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Whose idea was it to wait until the last minute?
Daisy looked at Kit.
Kit studied the porch railing with great interest.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Mmhm.
The sound covered everything that needed saying.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Get inside and change. Kit, you want to call your mama from here?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: No ma'am, she's at work.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: Your grandma home?

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yes ma'am.

blocks.speaker: Mrs. Jones
blocks.content: All right. You ride careful — there are puddles on the corner.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Yes ma'am.
He waited while Daisy walked her bike up to the porch. She turned at the top of the steps.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Tomorrow.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Tomorrow.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Don't go to the Henderson property without me.
He hadn't said anything about the Henderson property.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I wasn't—

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Without me.
She went inside. The screen door closed.
He pushed off on his bike. Smiled at the road.
